target.name: RoboTwin
target.founder: Megi Mejdrechová, Ladislav Dvořák, David Polák

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
RoboTwin is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised €2.3M in EIC funding. The comparable universe consists of 9 peers: 1 public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector comprises 11 documented peers.

Peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable to RoboTwin. Despite identifying 11 same-sector peers, none satisfied the narrow filtering criteria required for robust comparison: zero peers exist at the same seed stage, zero at adjacent development stages, and zero within similar valuation tiers. This scarcity prevents construction of a sufficiently populated comparable set (minimum three datapoints required) that would meaningfully triangulate fair value.

Without sufficient stage-matched or tier-matched peers, traditional multiples-based methods cannot be reliably applied. The data does not provide revenue, EBITDA, or other operational metrics for RoboTwin or comparables, precluding DCF or earnings multiple approaches. Given the early-stage nature and limited public disclosure typical of seed companies, a defensible valuation range cannot be established from the available data. Key uncertainties include the absence of operational metrics, the sparse peer set at seed stage in this sector, and the heterogeneity of the broader robotics landscape. Further diligence on RoboTwin's technology differentiation, market opportunity sizing, and team execution capability would be necessary to support a valuation conclusion.
